regions.text: A windmill works similarly, using wind to turn sails. Without wind, the miller cannot turn the millstones to make flour.
An old song tells of a lonely miller: 'I care for nobody, no not I, and nobody cares for me.'
He meant he did not love or fear anyone, and had no hopes. (Illustration: A windmill with rotating sails).
push: give a push. hope: desire for something. fear: opposite of hope. FUTURE: will push, hope, fear. PRESENT: push(es), hope(s), fear(s). PAST: pushed, hoped, feared.
